text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. In other words, you make the code cleaner and easier to understand, but it still does exactly the same thing as before. Refactoring focuses on improving readability, such as by renaming unclear variables or breaking long functions into smaller pieces. This helps reduce technical debt — the extra work caused by messy or poorly organized code. By regularly refactoring, developers maintain a healthy codebase, making future changes faster and less error-prone.
